pick.rationale: Ohio State holds a slight edge in this tightly contested 8/9 seed matchup, primarily due to TCU's uncertainty surrounding center Eddie Lampkin Jr., who stepped away from the team during the Big 12 Tournament for personal matters and remains a questionable availability for the NCAA Tournament. The Buckeyes feature superior offensive firepower with Bruce Thornton averaging 20.1 points per game alongside supporting scorers John Mobley Jr. and Devin Royal, while recent reports suggest Ohio State is getting healthier at the right time. Although TCU boasts strong defensive efficiency ranking 30th in Torvik, their mediocre offense ranked 81st-83rd combined with the potential absence of their key interior presence gives Ohio State the narrow advantage in what should be a competitive game.

reasoningStep.title: Ohio State vs TCU 8/9 Seed Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Ohio State edges TCU based on health advantages, offensive firepower, and TCU's key player uncertainty
reasoningStep.evidence: TCU ranks 43rd in KenPom and 48th in Torvik with strong defense (30th) but mediocre offense (81st-83rd). Ohio State showed KenPom rankings in the 27-34 range during the season. Eddie Lampkin Jr. stepped away from TCU during the Big 12 Tournament for personal matters, creating uncertainty about his NCAA Tournament availability. Ohio State's Bruce Thornton (20.1 PPG), John Mobley Jr., and Devin Royal (14.0 PPG) provide balanced scoring, and recent reports indicate the Buckeyes are getting healthier for the postseason. TeamRankings listed Ohio State as a 2.5-point favorite in this matchup.

result.title: Could Ohio State basketball get back to full health before postseason?
result.snippet: It's been nearly two months since the Ohio State men's basketball team started and finished a game with all its primary rotation players available. On Jan. 2, the Buckeyes resumed Big Ten play with an 80-73 road win against Rutgers.
